key_evidence: Texas AG and ten other states sued State Street on 2026-07-17 alleging ESG-driven coal market manipulation; the claim is unproven and no merits ruling exists.
Vanguard settled its portion of the same case for $29.5 million; the case against State Street and BlackRock continues.
State Street Global Advisors removed its board gender-diversity requirement from its 2025 proxy voting policy after the US election, stating its role is "not to be prescriptive."
Offsetting positive evidence: $26.8 million in State Street Foundation giving in 2025, $18.9 million to education and employment nonprofits, and 136,655 employee volunteer hours.

SCREENING RESULT: the published 60.2 was set 2026-04-20 by a documented baseline assessment that already considered the DEI retreat and layoff evidence. The -12.7 gap is assessor variance, not new conduct. Routed to calibration review, NOT proposed as a downgrade.

calibration_note: DO NOT APPLY AS A ONE-OFF DOWNGRADE. State Street's published 60.2 is NOT a bulk-import placeholder. It was set on 2026-04-20 by a documented first-time baseline assessment that already corrected the score down 32.3 points from an erroneous 92.5, and that assessment explicitly weighed BOTH the board-diversity retreat AND the Glassdoor layoff-culture evidence that drive this reassessment's lower Integrity and Boundaries scores. The only genuinely NEW within-window evidence is the 2026-07-17 Texas-led antitrust suit, which is an unproven allegation, not a finding — and under the benchmark's own allegation-versus-ruling standard it should carry limited weight before a court decides. A -12.7 point move three months after a recent, evidence-based baseline, on substantially the same evidence, is far more likely to be assessor-to-assessor variance than a real change in State Street's conduct. Per screening rule 5, that is a calibration concern and must be routed to review, not encoded as a one-off reset. Recommendation is flag-for-review, and the published 60.2 should stand until a reviewer reconciles the two assessments.
